title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Minnesota
description: This race is for Minnesota’s Class II U.S. Senate seat, currently held by Sen. Tina Smith, who announced she will not seek re-election. It is an open-seat contest in a state that has leaned Democratic in recent federal elections, but Republicans have narrowed margins in Minnesota in recent years, making the race politically important for both parties. The Democratic field has featured Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan and Rep. Angie Craig, while the Republican field includes several declared candidates such as Adam Schwarze, Christopher Brooks, Raymond Petersen, and others. The main contrasts so far are experience in elected office versus outsider or nontraditional backgrounds, along with differences over economic policy, public safety, and national partisan direction.
polling_note: No candidate-specific public head-to-head polling for the Minnesota U.S. Senate race was confirmed from credible sources in the available search results; one broader Minnesota 2026 poll was found but did not provide a Senate matchup in the accessible snippet.

candidates.name: Peggy Flanagan
candidates.summary: Peggy Flanagan is Minnesota’s lieutenant governor and a former state representative. Her campaign presents her as a progressive Democrat focused on working families, rights, and economic opportunity, and she has emphasized her background as an organizer and her Indigenous identity in public messaging.

summary_sources.title: Peggy Flanagan for Minnesota | Democrat for U.S. Senate

career_history.title: State Representative
career_history.description: Represented a Minnesota House district before becoming lieutenant governor.

career_history.title: Organizer / advocacy leader
career_history.description: Worked in organizing and advocacy before elected office.

candidates.name: Adam Schwarze
candidates.summary: Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine infantry veteran running as a Republican in Minnesota’s Senate race. His campaign emphasizes service, leadership, and a conservative approach to government, presenting him as an outsider with military experience rather than a career politician.

summary_sources.title: Conservative Leadership for a Stronger America - Adam Schwarze
Adam Schwarze: Minnesota Veteran for U.S. Senate
SCHWARZE FOR SENATE - committee overview - FEC

career_history.title: Navy SEAL
career_history.description: Campaign biography describes him as a former Navy SEAL.

career_history.title: Marine infantry veteran
career_history.description: Campaign biography describes him as a former Marine infantry servicemember.

candidates.name: Christopher Brooks
candidates.summary: Christopher Brooks is a Republican candidate in Minnesota’s 2026 U.S. Senate race. Publicly available search results identify him as a non-incumbent candidate, but the accessible campaign materials in search results provide limited biographical detail compared with some other candidates.

summary_sources.title: Meet Chris - Brooks for U.S. Senate

candidates.name: Raymond Petersen
candidates.summary: Raymond Petersen is a Republican candidate for Minnesota’s U.S. Senate seat and describes himself as a common-sense conservative and independent Republican. His campaign materials emphasize anti-establishment themes, term limits, and opposition to what he characterizes as entrenched or “bought and paid for” politics.

summary_sources.title: Raymond Petersen for US Senator from Minnesota
About the Campaign — Raymond Petersen for US Senator from Minnesota

career_history.title: Candidate / community resident
career_history.description: Campaign biography says he is a lifelong Minnesota resident; no prior elected office was identified in the accessible sources.

candidates.name: Tina Smith
candidates.summary: Tina Smith is the incumbent U.S. senator from Minnesota and announced she will not seek re-election in 2026. Before the Senate, she served as Minnesota’s lieutenant governor and worked in business and public service roles; her Senate biography highlights a focus on health care, education, and family economic issues.

summary_sources.title: U.S. Senator Tina Smith Pens Op-Ed Announcing She Will Not Seek Re-election in 2026
